title: Feature stores (Feast, Tecton) + point-in-time correctness + DSA Adv Graphs
focus: Training/serving skew, offline/online stores, PIT joins.

items.label: Why feature stores: training/serving skew, reuse
interviewQuestions: What is training/serving skew and how does a feature store eliminate it?
When is a feature store overkill?

items.label: Feast, Tecton, Vertex Feature Store
interviewQuestions: Walk through how Feast separates the offline and online stores.
Compare Feast vs Tecton vs Vertex Feature Store — when does each fit?

items.label: Point-in-time correctness
interviewQuestions: Why is point-in-time correctness critical for training data, and how does feature store handle it?
Walk me through how a leak from forward-looking features actually breaks model rollout.

tracks.label: Production ML · Data contracts and feature governance

items.label: Data contracts for model inputs, labels, and features
interviewQuestions: What should be in a data contract between an upstream events team and an ML training pipeline?
Which schema changes should block retraining immediately, and which should only warn?
How do you assign ownership when a critical feature silently changes meaning?

items.label: Schema evolution, backfills, and point-in-time feature correctness
interviewQuestions: How do you evolve a feature schema without breaking old training jobs or online inference?
What backfill checks catch point-in-time leakage before a feature goes live?
How would you prove an offline feature calculation matches the online feature service?
